mission.title: Install and Configure Buildpacks on Kubernetes
mission.description: Buildpacks is a CLI tool for building applications using Cloud Native Buildpacks, allowing developers to convert code into runnable images easily. Installing Buildpacks enables streamlined application development and deployment processes.

steps.title: Create a namespace for Buildpacks
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for the Buildpacks installation to isolate its resources.
```bash
kubectl create namespace buildpacks
```

steps.title: Deploy Buildpacks using kubectl
steps.description:
Apply the Buildpacks deployment configuration to your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/buildpacks/pack/releases/download/v0.40.1/pack.yaml --namespace buildpacks
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Buildpacks pod is running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace buildpacks
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Buildpacks deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/pack --namespace buildpacks --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Access Buildpacks CLI
steps.description:
To use the Buildpacks CLI, you may want to port-forward the service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/pack --namespace buildpacks 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Buildpacks deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the Buildpacks deployment from your cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete deployment pack --namespace buildpacks
```

uninstall.title: Clean up resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any associated resources such as services and config maps.
```bash
kubectl delete service pack --namespace buildpacks
kubectl delete configmap pack-config --namespace buildpacks
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Buildpacks namespace
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the namespace to clean up all resources associated with Buildpacks.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace buildpacks
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace buildpacks -o yaml > buildpacks-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Buildpacks deployment
upgrade.description:
Apply the updated Buildpacks deployment configuration for the new version.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/buildpacks/pack/releases/download/v0.40.1/pack.yaml --namespace buildpacks
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Buildpacks pod is updated and running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace buildpacks
```

upgrade.title: Rollback if necessary
upgrade.description:
If the upgrade fails, restore the previous configuration from the backup.
```bash
kubectl apply -f buildpacks-backup.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may indicate insufficient resources in the cluster. Check the events for the pod.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace buildpacks
```

troubleshooting.title: Buildpacks CLI not responding
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the service is running and accessible. Check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get services --namespace buildpacks
```

troubleshooting.title: Error during deployment
troubleshooting.description:
Check the logs of the Buildpacks pod for any errors during startup.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace buildpacks
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Buildpacks will result in a running pod in the 'buildpacks' namespace, and you should be able to access the Buildpacks CLI through the port-forwarded service. Ensure that the pod status is 'Running' and that there are no errors in the logs.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl installed to interact with it.
